entry.title: Managing Qualitative Metrics in SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) for ESRS and BRSR Framework
entry.content:
In this blog, I will be outlining how we can set-up qualitative metrics by their ESG area in SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) from both ESRS (European Sustainability Reporting Standards) and BRSR (Business Responsibility & Sustainability Reporting) framework perspective.

As part of March 2025 release, SAP has released a new feature to create "Qualitative Metrics" by their ESG area. We can assign applicable sustainability matter and define disclosure references based on the applicable reporting standard version. Reference type Narrative, Semi-Narrative, or Boolean can be used to determine the form that the reporting should take.

My Business use case for Qualitative Metric:

From ESRS framework perspective, I have considered the disclosure requirement S1-14:Health and Safety Metrics - The undertaking shall disclose information on the extent to which its own workforce is covered by its health and safety management system and the number of incidents associated with work-related injuries, ill health and fatalities of its own workforce.

From BRSR framework perspective, I have considered Essential Indicator - 4 : Whether the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is applicable to the entity`s activities (Yes/No). If yes, whether the waste collection plan is in line with the extended producer responsibility(EPR) plan submitted to Pollution Control Boards? If not, provide steps taken to address the same, from Section-C, Principle-2 : Business should provide goods and services in a manner that is sustainable and safe.

Let us first understand the master data management to establish qualitative metrics from ESRS and BRSR framework perspective.

Master Data Management:

Disclosure Requirement:

Reporting Standard:

Sustainability Matter:

Reporting Standard Version:

Data Point:

Data Point for ESRS S1-14, AR 81

BRSR Section-C,Principle-2, Essential Indicators 2a,2b,3 & 4

Now, let us create Qualitative Metric for ESRS ,S1-14 : Health and Safety Management System for own workforce.

Use "Manage Metrics" application to create a qualitative metric.

Specify the Metric ID, Metric Name, ESG Area, Sustainability Matter, and  Metric Description.

Specify the Disclosure References : Reporting Standard, Disclosure Requirement, and Data Point.

Specify the Assurance parameters.

Specify the Reference Types Definition and save the metric.

Now, let us use "Manage Qualitative ESG Data" application to upload the data for the qualitative metric that we have created, i.e., "Occupational health and safety management system". 

Use " Add Data" option to provide the disclosure information.

Choose the metric id and select the period from the dropdown value and provide the disclosure narrative to report out.

Now, let us create and provide disclosure information for BRSR (Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting) Qualitative Metric : Section-C, Principle-2, Essential Indicator - 4 : Whether the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is applicable to the entity`s activities (Yes/No). If yes, whether the waste collection plan is in line with the extended producer responsibility(EPR) plan submitted to Pollution Control Boards? If not, provide steps taken to address the same.

Use "Manage Metrics" application to create qualitative metric - Specify the Metric ID, Metric Name, ESG Area, Sustainability Matter, and  Metric Description.

Specify the Disclosure References : Reporting Standard, Disclosure Requirement, and Data Point.

Specify the Assurance parameters.

Specify the Reference Types Definition and save the metric.

Now, let us use "Manage Qualitative ESG Data" application to upload the data for the qualitative metric that we have created, i.e., "Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is applicable to the entity`s activities (Yes/No)".

Select the Metric Id and period from the dropdown values and provide the disclosure narrative to report out.

The summary of Qualitative Metrics published for a specific period.

😊This brings me to the end of process walkthrough on "Manage Qualitative Metrics" in SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT). I hope my blog proves useful to Sustainability Control Tower team. I believe that this qualitative metric management brings a significant value addition to the business, especially when adopting sustainability frameworks, such as ESRS , BRSR etc.

If you want to keep up with the key aspects of SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT), SAP EHSM : Environment Management, as well as their seamless integration, check out my SAP Blogs mentioned below.

  1. European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) 
  2. EHS - Environment Management, Emission Management Part-1 
  3. EHS - Environment Management, Emission Management Part-2 
  4. EHS - Environment Management, Emission Management Part-3 
  5. SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHS Environment Management 
  6. Addendum to SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHSM-EM 
  7. SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) Metric Management-Basic Custom Metric & Standard Custom Metric 
  8. Exporting and Importing Custom Objects and CPE Packages in SAP Sustainability Control Tower 

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

entry.title: 1H 2025 New and Upgraded Transactional Use Cases in Joule in Talent space
entry.content:
New Transactional Use Cases in Joule

Performance & Goals:
1.Create Activity – Allows users to create activities for themselves and for others that they share a channel with. Users can also link the activities to relevant performance and development goals.
2.View Performance Goals – Access and view detailed performance goals, including name, plan, and status. Navigate to the goal details for more insights.
3.Edit Performance Goals – Edit performance goals by adjusting status and completion percentage. Currently only these two fields are supported.
4.Add Comments to Performance Goals – Facilitate collaboration and feedback by adding comments to performance goals.

Succession & Development:
1.View Development Goals – View development goals, including the goal name, goal plan name, and goal status, and follow a link to the goal details page for more information.
2.Edit Development Goals – Allows users to edit the status and completion percentage of development goals. Currently only these two fields are supported.
3.Add Comments to Development Goals – Allows users to add comments to development goals.

Upgraded Existing Transactional Use Cases in Joule


Opportunity Marketplace:
1.Approve assignment offers
2.Approve assignment acceptance
3.Approve assignment applications
4.Approve assignment publishing

Performance & Goals:
1.Give feedback
2.Request feedback
3.Edit performance goals

Succession & Development:
1.View talent pool nominee approvals
2.Approve mentors
3.Approve mentoring requests
4.View and edit development goals
5.Add comments to development goals

For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

Thanks

Mridul

entry.title: Re: BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE
entry.content:
Hello @dchandra 

That is how BAPIs are typically developed. They give transaction control to the client, so the client (the caller of a BAPI) can decide to commit changes or roll them back. See SAP Help Transaction Model for Developing BAPIs

Thus, you need to call BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT after BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE to persist the material document. Also notice that you can't call BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE more then once without either BAPI_TRANSATION_COMMIT or BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK between the calls. That is one of the peculiarities of  BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski
